name: Org-mode MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server for Emacs org-mode — the powerful plain-text organizational system embedded in Emacs. Enables AI agents to read, write, and manage org-mode files including TODO items, headings, tags, deadlines, scheduled tasks, and org-mode outlines. Bridges AI capabilities with the org-mode productivity ecosystem.
use_cases: Reading and updating org-mode TODO lists and task states from productivity agents
Querying org-mode agenda and scheduled items for planning agents
Creating and organizing org-mode notes and project files from AI writing assistants
Integrating org-mode task management with AI-driven workflow automation
not_for: Teams using Obsidian, Notion, or other note-taking systems
Users unfamiliar with org-mode syntax and structure
best_when: An Emacs power user using org-mode for personal knowledge management, task tracking, and GTD wants AI agents to interact with their org files without leaving the org-mode ecosystem.
avoid_when: You don't use Emacs or org-mode. The org-mode format is specific to Emacs — other productivity systems won't benefit from this server.

auth.notes: No authentication — operates on local org-mode files. File system access controls apply.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free — operates on local org files
pricing.notes: Org-mode is free (part of Emacs). MCP server is free open source. No services required.

performance.rate_limits: No rate limits — local file operations

known_agent_gotchas: Org-mode syntax is complex — agents must understand org format for correct file manipulation
Concurrent edits with Emacs can cause conflicts — ensure Emacs isn't editing same files
Org-mode features (agenda, babel, etc.) require Emacs to evaluate — MCP server handles file I/O only
Niche tool — primarily for Emacs power users familiar with org-mode ecosystem
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local org files may contain personal/sensitive information — apply filesystem permissions. No network exposure.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local files. Personal org data may be sensitive. Filesystem permissions apply. No network.
